talk_metadata.talk_title: Chromatin remodeler CHD7 drives lineage plasticity and modulates replication competence in neuroendocrine prostate cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we haven't defined the specific upstream regulator for CHD7"
Speaker said: "we do have plenty results to suggest that they're not directly regulated by AR"

key_data_gaps: No in vivo therapeutic targeting of CHD7 demonstrated
Lack of patient stratification biomarkers for CHD7‑high NEPC

crispro_opportunity.description: CHD7 expression defines a subset of NEPC patients with ATR‑checkpoint dependence, but no clinical assay exists.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "CHD7 is an early event of the NEPC development" and "knockdown increased sensitivity to cisplatin"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO could develop a CHD7‑based predictive model integrating ATAC‑seq and expression to identify patients likely to respond to ATR‑checkpoint inhibition combined with platinum therapy.

crispro_opportunity.description: Targeting the CHD7‑ATR axis with checkpoint inhibitors may synergize with cisplatin in NEPC.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker observed that "combination leads to increased DNA accumulation and also apoptosis" when CHD7 knockdown was paired with cisplatin.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can prioritize ATR or CHK1 inhibitors as rational partners for CHD7‑high NEPC based on pathway modeling.

unresolved_questions: What upstream factors drive CHD7 transcription in early NEPC?
Can CHD7 inhibition be achieved pharmacologically?

assets_to_track: ATR checkpoint inhibitors in NEPC trials

talk_metadata.talk_title: Essential role of KDM5B‑SOX9 signaling in therapy‑induced neuroendocrine prostate cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we don't have directly evidence to confirm SOX9 really contribute"
Speaker said: "the incidence of NEPC is only 15% to 5% around the world"

key_data_gaps: Lack of in vivo efficacy data for KDM5B inhibition
Unclear whether SOX9 is sufficient to drive NEPC in vivo

crispro_opportunity.description: KDM5B inhibitors under development could be repurposed for NEPC if biomarker (KDM5B high / SOX9 activation) stratification is applied.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker demonstrated that "KDM5B upregulation could promote NE transformation through activating SOX9" and knockout reduced NE markers.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate KDM5B expression and SOX9 transcriptional signatures to select patients for KDM5B‑targeted trials.

unresolved_questions: What co‑factors cooperate with SOX9 to induce NE phenotype?
Does KDM5B inhibition reverse established NEPC in vivo?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Cooperation of DNMT and EZH2 in prostate cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the regulation or perturbing the regulators of these repressive mechanisms do differ functionally specific to the disease state"
Speaker said: "we haven't done it in the natural condition of NEPC development"

key_data_gaps: No clinical trial data linking EZH2 inhibition to methylation shifts in patients
Unclear therapeutic window for combined DNMT/EZH2 targeting

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine EZH2 inhibitors with DNMT1 degraders to exploit compensatory epigenetic rewiring in NEPC.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed that EZH2 deletion caused DNA methylation changes and DNMT1 inhibition led to H3K27 redistribution.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synergistic epigenetic network effects to prioritize combination regimens.

crispro_opportunity.description: EZH2 inhibitors that failed as monotherapy in NEPC may be rescued by pairing with DNMT1 inhibitors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that "EZH2 is very highly expressed in NEPC" and deletion altered methylome.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can identify patient subsets where EZH2 inhibition restores methylation patterns amenable to DNMT1 blockade.

cited_competitors.name: EZH2 inhibitors (e.g., tazemetostat)
cited_competitors.context: mentioned as existing agents targeting EZH2 in NEPC

cited_competitors.name: Decitabine
cited_competitors.context: used as DNMT inhibitor in preclinical models

unresolved_questions: What determines whether DNMT1 loss leads to up‑ or down‑regulation of neuroendocrine genes?
Can timing of EZH2 vs DNMT1 inhibition be optimized for maximal tumor suppression?

assets_to_track: EZH2 inhibitor clinical trials in prostate cancer

talk_metadata.talk_title: Targeting the ubiquitin reading function of DNMT1 as a next generation approach for epigenetic cancer therapy
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we are still trying to refine these binders"

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed that the ubiquitin interacting motifs (UIMs) of DNMT1 are essential, suggesting a narrow targetable pocket that competitors could design around.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Current DNMT inhibitors are replication‑dependent and have dose‑limiting toxicities; the new approach may require novel delivery or PK optimization.

key_data_gaps: No in vivo efficacy or safety data for the ubiquitin‑reader binders

crispro_opportunity.description: UIM‑targeting offers a replication‑independent DNMT1 inhibition strategy that could overcome limitations of 5‑azacytidine.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted that "the UIM is essential for DNA methylation maintenance" and that "this approach is not replication‑dependent".
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can benchmark the UIM platform against existing DNMTis using computational binding and off‑target prediction.

crispro_opportunity.description: Design early‑phase trials enrolling patients with DNMT1‑UHRF1 axis activation regardless of proliferation rate.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that "current DNMTis require replication" whereas the new binders do not.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate enrichment strategies based on UHRF1 expression signatures.

cited_competitors.name: 5‑Azacytidine / Decitabine
cited_competitors.context: discussed as existing DNMT inhibitors with limitations

unresolved_questions: Will inhibition of the UIM affect non‑cancerous cells with high replication rates?
Can resistance emerge via alternative DNMT1 recruitment mechanisms?

talk_metadata.talk_title: KDM2A driven epigenetic reprogramming promotes Barrett esophagus to adenocarcinoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "KDM2A was found to be increased and had a trend, but not very significant"
Speaker said: "we are still working on CUT&RUN data to show direct binding"

key_data_gaps: Direct ChIP/CUT&RUN evidence for KDM2A binding to oncogenic loci missing
No functional in vivo model demonstrating KDM2A inhibition prevents Barrett‑to‑EAC progression

crispro_opportunity.description: Existing KDM2A inhibitors could be repurposed for Barrett‑esophagus patients with high KDM2A expression.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed that "KDM2A overexpression in normal esophageal cells increased oncogenic signaling".
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate KDM2A expression with enhancer‑acetylation signatures to select candidates for early‑intervention trials.

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a KDM2A‑high biomarker panel to identify Barrett patients at risk of progression.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted a trend of increasing KDM2A from Barrett to EAC and correlation with H3K27ac elevation.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model risk scores using KDM2A levels, H3K36me2 loss, and H3K27ac gain.

unresolved_questions: What transcription factors cooperate with KDM2A to open MYC enhancers?
Can pharmacologic KDM2A inhibition reverse established EAC in vivo?

talk_metadata.talk_title: KMT2D is a master epigenetic regulator of lineage plasticity in lung squamous cell carcinoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we did not actually see a significant difference at first for the overall survival"
Speaker said: "the catalytic region is important because that's the site where the protein does the enhancer regulator work"

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker emphasized that loss‑of‑function mutations in the catalytic region drive poorer survival, indicating a narrow functional hotspot.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Lack of selective KMT2D inhibitors; current epigenetic drugs target broader H3K4 methylation pathways.

key_data_gaps: No pharmacologic inhibition of KMT2D demonstrated
Limited validation of FOXA1‑SOX2 interplay in human LUSC samples

crispro_opportunity.description: Targeting the KMT2D‑FOXA1‑SOX2 enhancer axis could provide a more precise approach than pan‑H3K4 inhibitors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed physical interaction between KMT2D and FOXA1 and redistribution of MEN1 in KMT2D‑null cells.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model disruption of the KMT2D‑FOXA1 complex and predict downstream transcriptional collapse in squamous lineage.

crispro_opportunity.description: MEN1 inhibitor (MI‑503) showed increased sensitivity in KMT2D‑null cells; combining with FOXA1 degraders may enhance efficacy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker reported that "mutant cell lines are more sensitive to this MEN1 inhibitor compared to wild type".
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can prioritize dual‑target regimens based on synthetic lethality scores.

cited_competitors.name: MI‑503 (MEN1 inhibitor)
cited_competitors.context: tested in KMT2D‑null lung cancer models

unresolved_questions: Is the catalytic activity of KMT2D required for the observed lineage switch, or does scaffolding suffice?
Can FOXA1 degradation reverse squamous transdifferentiation in vivo?
